name: SWEET PEPPER FAJITAS
description: We challenged ourselves to create the ultimate healthy fajita and we think this combination of peppers, beans, mushrooms and salsa hits the spot. It's a delicious Spanish-inspired fried pepper dish that works really well as part of a bigger meal or platter that would please a crowd. It's great as a lunch dish or even in a packed lunch!
ingredients: 1 x 400g tin of chopped tomatoes, drained
1 lime juiced and finely grated zest
1 x portion Guacamole (shop-bought or see page 194)
1 x portion salsa (shop-bought or see page 195)
method: Preheat the oven to 150°C | Large frying pan on a medium heat

Add a little oil to the frying pan. Peel and roughly chop the onion and add them to the pan. Cut the peppers in half, cut out the stems and seeds, slice the flesh into long thin strips and add to the pan. Sprinkle over the chilli powder, paprika and a pinch of salt.

Put the chili powder, sugar, cumin, paprika, cayenne pepper, garlic powder and a pinch of salt and pepper. Stir to mix well from the pan over the peppers for about 8 minutes, tossing regularly to cook for 15 minutes until soft, stirring regularly so that the peppers don't stick to the pan.

Meanwhile, drain the kidney beans and tip them into a serving bowl. Chop a third of the coriander and add them to the beans. Roughly chop the remaining coriander and put them on a plate. Spoon the remaining rice into another bowl. Spoon the guacamole and salsa into separate serving bowls.
Put the tortillas onto an ovenproof plate or baking tray. Cover with foil and put in the oven for 5 minutes to warm through.
The peppers are ready when they look well fried but not blackened, soft and just that sweet and delicious. Tip half into the serving bowl.
Take the tortillas out of the oven and transfer them to a serving plate.
Get the limes, wash them and simply chop the remaining coriander leaves. Fill a bowl with the tortilla chips.
Take all the separate bowls to the table. Fill the fajitas with delicious dollops of everything and roll them up to enjoy!
